Okay, so given what we just discussed, something I wanted to touch on was promoting from within. Because I've had a lot of gym ones ask me about this and it goes very much in line with what I just said. So I just wanted to reiterate it. Okay. Given everything we just discussed, remember these key points. Okay. One, because somebody is already on your team, you could easily do a timed trial for this person to take on a role they desire. So you could say, I want you to take this on for a week. Here's what I would like you to accomplish in a week. I need X amount of sales, X amount of cards sent, X amount of, you know, internal place closed, whatever it may be. And then letting them know how long they're going to be doing this. So you have from Monday, this Monday to next Monday. These are the things you need to accomplish. This is a trial period. Second is interviewing the person as a normal candidate and putting them through the exact same process. Okay. I actually am in favor of doing one or the other or doing the interview prior to the timed trial. Okay. Because I think a lot of it is that you don't want someone to think that what got them to this level will get them to the next. And so it's like, hey, we take this very seriously who we put in this position. So I need you to go through the interview process like anyone else would. Okay. And last but not least, you need to interview outside candidates for perspective. Okay. So you don't know if this person is the best option if you're not exploring other options. And so, and also what if you do the time trial and nobody else or and they, and they fail and they don't do well and you need that position filled. You must be interviewing. Okay. And so make sure you have other candidates coming in, you're interviewing them and that you let this person know that you will be doing so and that you will make the best decision for the business.
